document.title: Codex Orientation for execution-satellite
document.purpose: Keep durable pointers and compact working notes for Codex while collaborating with Lion on execution-satellite.

lions-dictionary-for-llms.use: Conceptual orientation and Lion's recurring vocabulary; treat entries as compact anchors rather than exhaustive specifications.

coding-conventions.use: Apply when designing, implementing, or reviewing code for Lion.

stickerdb-overview.use: Orient Execution Satellite to the neighboring StickerDB application and its expected future communication with this project.

inputlog-play-command-and-report.use: Authoritative integration reference for invoking InputLog playback and interpreting its atomic JSON lifecycle/completion report.

lionscliapp-reference.use: Required framework for hosting, configuring, and running the Execution Satellite application.

document-writing-conventions.read-when: Before writing or substantially editing a document.

soft-spec-writing-conventions.read-when: Before writing or substantially editing a SoftSpec.

coding-style: Clarity beats cleverness; code should read like a story and expose its structure from a distance.
Prefer procedural functions, lightweight conventions, direct JSON-friendly structures, and minimal scaffolding.
Normalize and validate external input at castle gates; trust canonical structures inside.
Prefer few function arguments; contextual state may live in a small canonical g dictionary.
Avoid classes and threads by default unless they carry concrete weight.
Prefer UTF-8 JSON, JSONL for append-only event logs, atomic writes, and filesystem-native coordination where appropriate.
Preserve momentum, legibility, inspectability, and trust over theoretical purity.
dictionary-guidance: Use Lion's dictionary as semantic grounding, not as a complete specification.
Respect Lion's established vocabulary and distinctions rather than silently replacing them with conventional near-synonyms.
When an entry points to a full specification, seek that specification before making details load-bearing.
stickerdb-relationship: Execution Satellite orbits StickerDB: treat StickerDB as a neighboring application and likely future source or destination of communication.
StickerDB is a Lion Conventional Tkinter database editor whose data model uses M1 transport units.
StickerDB expects filesystem-native FileTalk communication through isolated inbox and outbox boundaries.
Do not infer a detailed message protocol from the overview; inspect the relevant specification or live artifacts when communication work begins.
execution-satellite-core: The satellite separates StickerDB's production intent from fragile GUI execution in Leonardo Design Studio through InputLog.
Execution is human-gated: receiving a request does not authorize immediate execution; the operator prepares the desktop and starts the run.
StickerDB owns each requester-side job cell and its cleanup. The satellite may read named inputs, write named outputs and response_path, but must not clean requester-owned files.
Requests and responses use absolute paths and stable job_id values. Requests include expires_at and response_path.
The initial job vocabulary is layout_sticker_to_lds and print_lds_file.
InputLog is blind. Any interruption or abnormal result stops the entire queue for operator choice: retry current job, fail current job, or fail the entire queue.
Responses report only what the satellite actually knows, distinguishing automation completion and observed files from business or physical-print certainty.
The recommended v1 submission model copies request JSON into the satellite inbox while retaining full paths back to the requester-owned job cell.
inputlog-staging: InputLog consumes one staged file from a dedicated launch folder, currently defaulting to C:\Users\Robert\Launch.
At the instant the operator says Go, the launch folder must exist and be completely empty. A nonblank folder is a hard safety stop and the satellite deletes nothing.
Blank-at-Go authorizes the satellite to own, clear, and reuse the launch folder contents for the duration of that launched queue, including cleanup after failures.
print_lds_file copies the requester LDS file into the launch folder, runs print-sticker, then clears the launch folder.
layout_sticker_to_lds copies the requester sticker image into the launch folder, runs layout, moves the configured Leonardo output file to the requester output path, then clears the launch folder.
Leonardo currently saves generated layouts as D:\tmp\Untitled.LDS; both the save folder and output filename are configurable.
inputlog-integration: Run InputLog with C:\lion\installed\inputlog as its default working directory; this is the universal installed InputLog project root containing the production recordings.
Use a fresh report path and wait for the InputLog process to exit before reading it.
Playback starts only after InputLog successfully creates the initial atomic JSON report.
Require a readable report for the expected recording and a sufficiently recent ts; do not infer success from stdout or process exit alone.
completion-status normal means the recording reached its end.
completion-status user-aborted means physical user input stopped playback; abort-index is diagnostic context.
completion-status error means a caught failure; inspect stage plus error.type and error.message.
completion-status running after process exit is unsuccessful or indeterminate, never success.
Missing, malformed, stale, mismatched, or unknown-status reports are unsuccessful or indeterminate.
Consumers should require the seven documented keys, tolerate unknown additional keys, and ignore JSON formatting and key order.
Physical alphabetic key presses request labels rather than aborting; other relevant physical keyboard or mouse input aborts playback.
labels-added may indicate that InputLog rewrote the selected recording during the run.
lionscliapp-hosting: The Execution Satellite app must use lionscliapp for application hosting, persistent configuration, CLI parsing, command dispatch, and lifecycle.
Declare the application, project directory, configuration keys, descriptions, and commands before calling app.main() as the final entrypoint operation.
Use app.ctx only during command execution; it is empty during declaration.
Prefer projpath.* for satellite-owned files under its hidden project directory and execpath.* for paths resolved from the invocation execution root.
Do not introduce new path.* declarations; that namespace is deprecated.
Remember that general CLI/config values remain strings and must be explicitly coerced when another type is needed.
Use lionscliapp's layered configuration: declared defaults, persisted config.json, options file, then CLI overrides.
Use built-in set, get, keys, and help behavior rather than recreating configuration commands.
Keep command handlers as no-argument functions reading the plain global app.ctx dictionary.
Use lionscliapp JSON/path helpers where they fit, while preserving any stronger atomic-write requirements imposed by the satellite protocol.
